reasoning: Mongolia is exceptionally stable with virtually zero instances of fatal political violence or conflict that would be recorded by ACLED. The reference value is roughly 1.0, meaning to trigger a YES, over 10 fatalities must occur in a given 30-day period. Historically, even the most severe political unrest in Mongolia's modern history (the 2008 post-election riots) only resulted in 5 fatalities. Thus, the base rate of 11 or more fatalities occurring within any 30-day window is practically zero. Because we lack tools to identify any unprecedented immediate crisis, the probability falls well below the 0.02 minimum boundary. The first resolution outcome period largely overlaps with the already completed timeframe up to the October 26 cutoff. Obeying the constraint to increase uncertainty (pulling forecasts closer to 0.5 over longer horizons), I have scaled the estimates as 0.02, 0.03, and 0.04, respectively.
